title: Fast-Wheel Progressive Loading
description:
Replace single atomic uv pip install with a streaming, per-package pipeline that makes each package importable as soon as its wheel is safely committed. The app starts running at the same time as the first download.

Phase 1 is a hybrid installer: uv resolves and handles tiny/metadata-sensitive wheels, fast-wheel daemon handles medium/large wheels with progressive extraction and demand-driven priority.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] fast-wheel daemon extracts each wheel atomically into site-packages
- [ ] Lazy import hook blocks until wheel is committed (done/ marker)
- [ ] Demand file reprioritizes downloads based on actual import order
- [ ] Hybrid orchestrator coordinates uv + fast-wheel daemon
- [ ] Cold start benchmark shows material speedup over baseline uv install
- [ ] Warm path reuses cached artifact plans and completed environments

## Architecture
- Rust daemon reads JSON manifest, downloads/extracts progressively
- Atomic extraction: staging dir + rename, done/failed markers
- Demand watcher: file-based IPC, reserved slot for just-demanded packages
- Python orchestrator: uv resolve → classify → spawn daemon + hook → run script

## Plan Reference
- ../private-zerostart/planning/fast-wheel-progressive/README.md
- ../private-zerostart/planning/fast-wheel-progressive/01-architecture.md

title: Rust Atomic Extract + Status Markers
description:
Add atomic per-wheel extraction to fast-wheel. Each wheel extracts to a staging directory inside site-packages, then commits into place via rename. Status markers (done/, failed/) signal readiness to the import hook.

## Scope
- extract_wheel_atomic() — staging dir + rename commit
- stream_extract_wheel_atomic() — same for streaming path
- Status directory creation and done/failed marker writing
- Stale staging dir cleanup on startup

## Pre-conditions
None — this is foundational work on existing extract.rs and streaming.rs

## Plan Reference
- ../private-zerostart/planning/fast-wheel-progressive/02-fast-wheel-daemon.md (extract.rs, streaming.rs sections)
- ../private-zerostart/planning/fast-wheel-progressive/01-architecture.md (Extraction Target section)
close_reason: All 4 tasks complete. atomic extract, streaming atomic extract, status dir — 12 tests passing on macOS + Linux GPU pod.

title: Implement extract_wheel_atomic() with staging dir
description:
Add atomic extraction to extract.rs. Extract wheel contents into a staging directory (.installing-{pkg}-{random}/) inside site-packages, then rename each top-level entry into place.

## Files
- crates/zs-fast-wheel/src/extract.rs (modify)

## Approach
1. Create .installing-{pkg_name}-{random}/ staging dir inside site-packages
2. Extract wheel contents into staging dir (reuse existing extract logic)
3. Validate staged contents before exposure
4. Move each top-level entry from staging to site-packages via fs::rename
5. Refuse to overwrite existing paths (dedicated env assumption)
6. Remove empty staging dir after commit

## Plan Reference
- ../private-zerostart/planning/fast-wheel-progressive/02-fast-wheel-daemon.md (extract.rs section)
close_reason: extract_wheel_atomic() implemented with staging dir + rename commit. 2 tests passing locally + GPU pod.

title: Implement stream_extract_wheel_atomic() for large wheels
description:
Add atomic extraction to the streaming path in streaming.rs. Same staging-dir-then-rename pattern as extract_wheel_atomic() but for the Range-request streaming download path.

## Files
- crates/zs-fast-wheel/src/streaming.rs (modify)

## Approach
1. Create staging dir inside site-packages
2. Run existing streaming logic targeting staging dir instead of site-packages directly
3. After streaming completes, commit staged entries via rename
4. Same overwrite-refusal and cleanup as extract_wheel_atomic()

## Plan Reference
- ../private-zerostart/planning/fast-wheel-progressive/02-fast-wheel-daemon.md (streaming.rs section)
close_reason: stream_extract_wheel_atomic() implemented, reuses commit_staged(). Compiles and cross-compiles.

title: Add status directory with done/failed markers
description:
Create status directory infrastructure for per-package completion signaling. The import hook uses done/ and failed/ markers as authoritative readiness/failure signals.

## Files
- crates/zs-fast-wheel/src/pipeline.rs (modify — add status dir creation and marker writing)

## Approach
1. Create status_dir/installing sentinel at startup
2. Create status_dir/done/ and status_dir/failed/ directories
3. After successful wheel commit: touch status_dir/done/{distribution}
4. On failure: write error message to status_dir/failed/{distribution}
5. Remove status_dir/installing when all wheels are done
6. Clean up stale .installing-* staging dirs from interrupted runs on startup

## Plan Reference
- ../private-zerostart/planning/fast-wheel-progressive/02-fast-wheel-daemon.md (Status Directory section)
- ../private-zerostart/planning/fast-wheel-progressive/01-architecture.md (Per-Package Progress)
close_reason: StatusDir struct with done/failed markers, installing sentinel, demand path. 7 tests passing locally + GPU pod.

title: Rust Manifest Parsing + Priority Queue
description:
Add JSON manifest parsing and a priority queue for wheel scheduling. The manifest replaces CLI URL args as the input for daemon mode. The priority queue enables demand-driven reordering.

## Scope
- WheelSpec struct and manifest JSON deserialization
- InstallQueue with VecDeque, prioritize(), next(), mark_done()
- Default ordering: small wheels first, large last

## Pre-conditions
None — this is foundational data structures, parallel with atomic extract

## Plan Reference
- ../private-zerostart/planning/fast-wheel-progressive/02-fast-wheel-daemon.md (Manifest File, Priority Queue sections)

## Approach

2. Define Manifest: site_packages, status_dir, wheels: Vec<WheelSpec>
3. Implement Manifest::from_file(path) with serde_json
4. Validate required fields, handle missing optional fields

## Plan Reference

close_reason: Manifest JSON parsing with WheelSpec struct. 6 tests passing.

title: Implement InstallQueue with demand-driven prioritization
description:
Build the priority queue that orders wheel installation. Default: small first, large last. Supports demand-driven reprioritization via prioritize().

## Files
- crates/zs-fast-wheel/src/queue.rs (new)
- crates/zs-fast-wheel/src/main.rs (add mod queue)

## Approach
1. InstallQueue with pending (VecDeque), in_progress (HashSet), done (HashSet)
2. new(wheels) sorts by size ascending
3. prioritize(distribution) moves to front of pending (skip if done/in_progress)
4. next() pops from front, moves to in_progress
5. mark_done(distribution) moves from in_progress to done
6. is_empty() for termination check

## Plan Reference
- ../private-zerostart/planning/fast-wheel-progressive/02-fast-wheel-daemon.md (Priority Queue section)
close_reason: InstallQueue with size-sorted priority, demand-driven prioritize(), mark_done/failed. 7 tests passing.

title: Rust Daemon Mode + Demand Watcher
description:
Add daemon subcommand to fast-wheel that runs as a long-lived process, progressively downloading and extracting wheels. Includes demand file watcher for import-driven reprioritization and reserved slot scheduling.

## Scope
- daemon subcommand in main.rs (clap)
- daemon_run() orchestration in pipeline.rs
- DemandWatcher: polls demand file for new distribution IDs
- Reserved demand slot: one download slot kept available for just-demanded packages
- Lifecycle: read manifest → create status dirs → process wheels → remove installing → exit

## Pre-conditions
- Atomic extract (staging + commit) must be working
- Manifest parsing + priority queue must be working

## Plan Reference
- ../private-zerostart/planning/fast-wheel-progressive/02-fast-wheel-daemon.md (daemon_run, demand.rs, Lifecycle sections)
- ../private-zerostart/planning/fast-wheel-progressive/01-architecture.md (Demand-Driven Priority, Concurrency Model)
close_reason: Daemon mode complete. Cross-compiled binary tested on GPU pod — downloads real PyPI wheels progressively with demand watcher.

## Approach
1. Add Command enum with Install and Daemon variants
2. Daemon takes --manifest PathBuf
3. Install keeps existing args (wheels, target, parallel_downloads, stream, etc.)
4. Default (no subcommand) maps to Install for backwards compat
5. Daemon variant calls daemon_run() from pipeline.rs

## Plan Reference
- ../private-zerostart/planning/fast-wheel-progressive/02-fast-wheel-daemon.md (CLI, Backwards Compatibility)
close_reason: Daemon subcommand added to CLI with clap. Install subcommand preserves existing behavior.

title: Implement daemon_run() orchestration loop
description:
Implement the daemon's main orchestration loop in pipeline.rs. Reads manifest, processes wheels progressively with download + atomic extract + status markers.

## Files
- crates/zs-fast-wheel/src/pipeline.rs (modify)

## Approach
1. Parse manifest → create status dirs → clean stale staging dirs
2. Create InstallQueue from manifest wheels
3. Main loop: pop from queue → download → atomic extract → mark done
4. Download strategy by size: <1MB in-memory, 1-50MB temp file, >50MB streaming
5. Bounded concurrency via semaphore (existing pattern)
6. Reserve one slot for demand-responsive work
7. Remove installing sentinel when all done, exit

## Plan Reference
- ../private-zerostart/planning/fast-wheel-progressive/02-fast-wheel-daemon.md (daemon_run, Download Strategy Per Size)
close_reason: daemon_run() orchestrates manifest→queue→download→atomic extract→status markers. Tested with real PyPI wheels on GPU pod.

title: Implement DemandWatcher with reserved slot scheduling
description:
Add demand file watcher that reads priority signals from the import hook and reprioritizes the install queue. Keep one download slot reserved for demand-responsive work.

## Files
- crates/zs-fast-wheel/src/demand.rs (new)
- crates/zs-fast-wheel/src/pipeline.rs (integrate demand watcher into daemon_run)
- crates/zs-fast-wheel/src/main.rs (add mod demand)

## Approach
1. DemandWatcher: reads status_dir/demand file, tracks seen_lines
2. poll() returns newly demanded distribution IDs
3. Poll on 100ms interval (tokio::time::interval)
4. On new demand: call queue.prioritize(distribution)
5. Reserved slot: keep one semaphore permit unclaimed until demand arrives or queue nearly drained
6. Integration: spawn demand watcher as tokio task alongside download workers

## Plan Reference
- ../private-zerostart/planning/fast-wheel-progressive/02-fast-wheel-daemon.md (demand.rs, Priority Queue policy)
- ../private-zerostart/planning/fast-wheel-progressive/01-architecture.md (Demand-Driven Priority)
close_reason: DemandWatcher polls demand file for new distributions, prioritizes queue. 4 unit tests + integrated into daemon loop.

title: Python Resolver + Environment Cache
description:
Build the Python artifact resolution and environment caching layer. Uses uv pip compile to resolve exact artifacts with hash/index fidelity, then caches plans and completed environments for the warm path.

## Scope
- resolve_requirements() — uv pip compile → ArtifactPlan
- ArtifactPlan data model (urls, hashes, import roots, sizes, distribution names)
- Wheel classification: tiny (uv) vs medium/large (fast-wheel)
- generate_manifest() — write JSON manifest for daemon
- Environment cache: plan-keyed env reuse (.zerostart/plans/, .zerostart/envs/)
- Import name → distribution mapping for the hook

## Pre-conditions
None — parallel with Rust work

## Plan Reference
- ../private-zerostart/planning/fast-wheel-progressive/03-orchestrator.md (Resolution, Manifest Generation, Environment and Cache Model)
close_reason: resolver.py, cache.py, tests all implemented and passing

title: Implement ArtifactPlan resolution via uv pip compile
description:
Build the resolution layer that produces an exact artifact plan from requirements. Uses uv pip compile for resolution, then resolves exact wheel URLs with hash/index fidelity.

## Files
- python/zerostart/resolver.py (new)

## Approach
1. resolve_requirements(requirements, python_version, platform) → ArtifactPlan
2. Shell out to uv pip compile with --no-header --annotation-style=line
3. Parse resolved output into ArtifactPlan dataclass
4. ArtifactPlan contains: list of WheelArtifact (url, hash, distribution, import_roots, size)
5. Classify wheels: tiny (<1MB, uv handles) vs medium/large (fast-wheel handles)
6. Generate import_name → distribution mapping for the hook
7. Cache plan by hash(requirements + python_version + platform)

## Plan Reference
- ../private-zerostart/planning/fast-wheel-progressive/03-orchestrator.md (Resolution section)
close_reason: Implemented resolver.py, cache.py, test_resolver.py - all 11 tests passing

title: Implement environment cache with plan-keyed reuse
description:
Build environment caching so the warm path reuses completed environments instead of reinstalling.

## Files
- python/zerostart/cache.py (new)

## Approach
1. env_key = sha256(json(plan.cache_key_payload, sort_keys=True))
2. Layout: .zerostart/plans/{env_key}.json, .zerostart/envs/{env_key}/, .zerostart/envs/{env_key}.tmp/
3. If envs/{env_key}/ exists and marked complete → reuse immediately
4. If only plan exists → resume/complete the install
5. Build new envs in .tmp/ then finalize via rename
6. ensure_plan_environment(plan) → returns env path and is_complete flag

## Plan Reference
- ../private-zerostart/planning/fast-wheel-progressive/03-orchestrator.md (Environment and Cache Model)
close_reason: Implemented resolver.py, cache.py, test_resolver.py - all 11 tests passing

title: Python Orchestrator Integration
description:
Update the Python orchestrator (run.py) to coordinate uv + fast-wheel daemon in hybrid mode. Ties together resolution, manifest generation, daemon spawning, lazy import hook, and user script execution.

## Scope
- Updated run() flow: resolve → classify → spawn uv thread + daemon → install hook → exec script
- Warm path: detect completed env, skip install
- Entry point generation for fast-wheel-managed wheels
- sst auto-patch integration (sitecustomize.py)
- Cleanup: wait for daemon, remove status dir

## Pre-conditions
- Rust daemon mode must be working (daemon subcommand functional)
- Python resolver + cache must be working (artifact plan generation)

title: Update run.py with hybrid uv + fast-wheel daemon flow
description:
Rewrite the orchestrator to coordinate uv (tiny wheels) + fast-wheel daemon (medium/large wheels) in parallel, with lazy import hook.

## Files
- python/zerostart/run.py (modify)

## Approach
1. resolve → classify → check env cache (warm path: exec immediately)
2. Start uv thread for tiny wheels
3. Generate manifest, spawn zs-fast-wheel daemon subprocess
4. Install lazy import hook with import_name→distribution map
5. Run user script (exec compile)
6. Cleanup: remove hook, join uv thread, wait for daemon, remove status dir

title: PyO3 bindings for fast-wheel daemon
description:
Replace subprocess + filesystem IPC with direct PyO3 bindings. Python calls Rust daemon directly, signals demand via function call instead of file polling, and gets completion notifications instantly.

## Scope
- PyO3 module: zs_fast_wheel (Python-importable Rust extension)
- DaemonHandle: start daemon, signal demand, check/wait done
- Replace 100ms file polling with direct Rust→Python notification
- maturin build setup

## Why
- Eliminates subprocess spawn overhead
- Instant demand signaling (vs 100ms file poll)
- No JSON serialization for manifest (Python dict → Rust struct)
- Cleaner API for orchestrator

## Plan Reference
- ../private-zerostart/planning/fast-wheel-progressive/02-fast-wheel-daemon.md

title: Create PyO3 module with DaemonHandle
description:
Create a PyO3 Python extension module that exposes the daemon as a callable API instead of a subprocess.

## API
- zs_fast_wheel.start_daemon(manifest: dict) → DaemonHandle
- DaemonHandle.signal_demand(distribution: str) — instant priority bump
- DaemonHandle.is_done(distribution: str) → bool
- DaemonHandle.is_failed(distribution: str) → Optional[str]
- DaemonHandle.wait_done(distribution: str, timeout: float) → bool — blocks until done
- DaemonHandle.wait_all(timeout: float) → bool
- DaemonHandle.shutdown() — clean stop

## Files
- crates/zs-fast-wheel/src/lib.rs (new — PyO3 module)
- crates/zs-fast-wheel/Cargo.toml (add pyo3, lib target)
- pyproject.toml or maturin config

## Approach
- Reuse existing daemon.rs, queue.rs, manifest.rs internals
- DaemonHandle holds Arc<Mutex<InstallQueue>> + tokio runtime handle
- signal_demand() directly calls queue.prioritize() — no file IPC
- wait_done() uses tokio::sync::Notify or watch channel — no polling
close_reason: DaemonEngine with in-memory state + PyO3 DaemonHandle wrapper. 33 tests passing.

title: Add per-package completion notification channel
description:
Replace done/ file polling with a Rust notification channel that Python can wait on.

## Files
- crates/zs-fast-wheel/src/daemon.rs (modify — add notify on completion)
- crates/zs-fast-wheel/src/lib.rs (expose wait_done via channel)

## Approach
- Add tokio::sync::broadcast or watch channel for completion events
- Each wheel completion sends (distribution, Ok/Err) to channel
- Python wait_done() blocks on channel receiver filtered by distribution
- Keeps file-based done/ markers too (for CLI mode / debugging)
close_reason: Completion notification via tokio watch channels, integrated into DaemonEngine. No file-based IPC needed.

title: Update orchestrator to use PyO3 DaemonHandle
description:
Update run.py to import zs_fast_wheel and use DaemonHandle instead of subprocess.Popen.

## Files
- python/zerostart/run.py (modify)
- python/zerostart/lazy_imports.py (modify — use DaemonHandle for demand)

## Approach
- import zs_fast_wheel
- handle = zs_fast_wheel.start_daemon(manifest_dict)
- Hook calls handle.signal_demand(dist) instead of writing demand file
- Hook calls handle.wait_done(dist, timeout) instead of polling done/ dir
- Fallback: if PyO3 module not available, fall back to subprocess mode
close_reason: Rewrote run.py and lazy_imports.py to use DaemonHandle. No file-based IPC.

title: Validate: PyO3 bindings on GPU pod
description:
Validate PyO3 bindings work end-to-end on GPU pod.

## Test Commands
# Build wheel
cd crates && maturin develop --release

# Run Python tests
gpu run "cd crates && maturin develop --release && cd ../python && python -m pytest tests/test_pyo3.py -v"

## Testing Approach
- Unit test: start_daemon with small wheels, verify is_done after completion
- Unit test: signal_demand triggers priority change
- Unit test: wait_done blocks until wheel ready
- Integration: full orchestrator with PyO3 handle on GPU pod
- Verify: demand latency < 1ms (vs 100ms file polling)

## Expected Results
- All tests pass on GPU pod
- Measurable latency improvement for demand signaling
